author: Jonathan Simcoe
description: Turns block references into live mirrored transclusions with inline previews and editing. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: jdsimcoe

manifest.name: Mirror

name: Mirror

releases.author: jdsimcoe
releases.description:
## What's changed

Mirror 1.0.1 is a maintenance release focused on smoother local block-link creation and cleaner release validation.

### Fixed

- Fixed an issue where using the `@` block importer could create a block link that immediately rendered as "Block not found."
- Improved block resolution so freshly-created block IDs can be found even before Obsidian's metadata cache has fully caught up.
- Updated same-note `@` insertion to write through the active editor, avoiding editor/file state drift.

### Maintenance

- Removed marketplace warning patterns for `!important`, `:has(...)`, `document.createElement(...)`, and `instanceof HTMLElement`.
- Updated vulnerable transitive dev dependencies reported through `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd`.
- Rebuilt `main.js` from source for release verification.
- Added a GitHub Actions release workflow for future release asset provenance attestations.

## Install

Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` from this release, or update through Obsidian once available.
releases.name: Mirror 1.0.1

releases.author: jdsimcoe
releases.description:
Mirror 1.0.0 is the initial public release of the plugin. It brings block transclusion workflows into everyday writing in Obsidian with three core features:

- Embed transcluded block references directly in your notes with a richer Live Preview experience and a simplified Reading View rendering
- Copy embed-ready block references from linked mentions in the backlinks sidebar, generating a block ID automatically when needed
- Use `@mention` search to find matching text anywhere in your vault and quickly insert a block reference into the current note

This release also includes:
- Inline editing for mirrored blocks
- Local-first behavior with no external services
- Updated documentation, screenshots, and release assets for community plugin submission
